overview.short: Malkheda originally known as Manyakheta (IAST: Mānyakheṭa, Prakrit: "Mannakheḍa"), and also known as Malkhed, is a town in Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Malkheda originally known as Manyakheta (IAST: Mānyakheṭa, Prakrit: "Mannakheḍa"), and also known as Malkhed, is a town in Karnataka, India. It is located on the banks of Kagina river in Sedam Taluk of Kalaburagi district, around 40 km from Kalaburagi. The city reached the peak of its prosperity during the 9th and 10th centuries, serving as the Imperial capital of the Rashtrakutas and historical heart of Jainism where most Jain present at the time. At Manyakheta, there is a historical fort whose restoration is in progress based on a proposal submitted by HKADB (Hyderabad Karnataka Area Develop

topPlaces.name: Malkhed Fort
topPlaces.description: The Malkhed Fort, (also known as the Manyakheta Fort) is located on the banks of the Kagina River in the district of Gulbarga in the state of Karnataka, India. Its construction attributed to the Rashtrakuta Emperor Amoghavarsha I. Manyakheta was the capital of the Rashtrakuta Empire from 814 AD to 968 AD, and after they were overthrown, it became the capital of the Western Chalukya Empire.

topPlaces.name: Dandothi
topPlaces.description: Dandothi is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Chitapur taluk of Kalaburagi district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Udagi
topPlaces.description: Udagi (also spelled as Oodagi) is a village in the Sedam taluk of Gulbarga district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

items.note: The Malkhed Fort, (also known as the Manyakheta Fort) is located on the banks of the Kagina River in the district of Gulbarga in the state of Karnataka, India. Its construction attributed to the Rashtrakuta Emperor Amoghavarsha I. Manyakheta was the capital of the Rashtrakuta Empire from 814 AD to 968 AD, and after they were overthrown, it became the capital of the Western Chalukya Empire.
Dandothi is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Chitapur taluk of Kalaburagi district in Karnataka.
Udagi (also spelled as Oodagi) is a village in the Sedam taluk of Gulbarga district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Manyakheta?
faq.a: The best time to visit Manyakheta is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (135 km) and the nearest railway station is Solapur Junction (143 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Manyakheta?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Manyakheta at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Manyakheta?
faq.a: Stays in Manyakheta start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Manyakheta?
faq.a: The top attractions are Malkhed Fort, Dandothi, Udagi.
